4ff0437a-4708-4a82-9ad1-3871f55b2fff.name: FDA mandatory recall authority (FSMA Section 423) — infant formula exception
4ff0437a-4708-4a82-9ad1-3871f55b2fff.note: FSMA gives FDA mandatory recall authority for FDA-regulated foods generally; infant formula recall authority is a separate, older mandatory mechanism under 21 CFR Part 107.

0b5a11ed-f395-4fa2-87f5-9387207c02fd.name: FDA shell egg vs. USDA egg products jurisdiction split
0b5a11ed-f395-4fa2-87f5-9387207c02fd.note: FDA regulates shell eggs; USDA FSIS regulates further-processed (liquid/frozen/dried) egg products.

37bf5ebf-47ce-4e8a-9052-93d8eade2b5f.name: FSIS recalls are voluntary, not mandatory
37bf5ebf-47ce-4e8a-9052-93d8eade2b5f.note: FSIS's own guidance confirms all its recalls are voluntary; its only compulsion lever on a refusing firm is DOJ referral for detention/seizure, not the power to directly order a recall.

bcf4d03b-bac6-4217-bb3d-5b04c24dd48f.name: FSIS generic label approval (label pre-approval now the exception)
bcf4d03b-bac6-4217-bb3d-5b04c24dd48f.note: Since the 2013/2023 rule changes, most meat/poultry labels are self-certified under generic label approval; FSIS pre-approval now applies only to special claims.

30e9f383-71e7-4b37-9c98-e6679c982f25.name: USDA AMS voluntary grading fee schedule
30e9f383-71e7-4b37-9c98-e6679c982f25.note: Confirms AMS grading (e.g. USDA Grade A, USDA Choice) is voluntary and fee-based.

54f9b1c2-339d-484d-a76b-ddb53931fc08.name: USDA AMS National Bioengineered Food Disclosure Standard

a513ecce-17b3-40de-97fe-330f48f3e669.name: EPA pesticide tolerances under FFDCA / Food Quality Protection Act
a513ecce-17b3-40de-97fe-330f48f3e669.note: EPA sets maximum pesticide residue tolerances; FDA/USDA enforce them.

bd24a8e8-29cd-4369-b0fb-5a0f34a6235d.name: EPA drinking water standards (Safe Drinking Water Act)
bd24a8e8-29cd-4369-b0fb-5a0f34a6235d.note: National Primary Drinking Water Regulations affecting food manufacturing water quality.

5270eda2-77d9-4497-bcf3-5c27e681c78a.name: TTB jurisdiction threshold — wine 7% ABV or greater
5270eda2-77d9-4497-bcf3-5c27e681c78a.note: TTB regulates wine at 7% ABV or greater (not "above 7%"); wine under 7% ABV falls to FDA. Also confirms beer under TTB requires both malted barley and hops.

1690475b-ce2c-48e8-97d8-c971136d1b9b.name: FTC/FDA food advertising vs. labeling jurisdiction split
1690475b-ce2c-48e8-97d8-c971136d1b9b.note: Confirms the long-standing FTC/FDA liaison agreement (originally 1971) dividing advertising (FTC) from labeling (FDA) jurisdiction.

18857130-e665-46e4-817a-bc9bca24767c.name: FDA Food Code adoption by states
18857130-e665-46e4-817a-bc9bca24767c.note: Confirms the Food Code's voluntary-model status and adoption in some form by ~97% of states/territories.

dae96c92-1d7e-463e-a37d-7c9c92177079.name: NIST Handbook 44 as weights-and-measures reference model
dae96c92-1d7e-463e-a37d-7c9c92177079.note: Federal reference model for weights-and-measures, enforced at state/local level.

b86367cd-92f4-4447-886a-12fc38f32196.name: CDC foodborne illness burden estimate (~48M/128,000/3,000)
b86367cd-92f4-4447-886a-12fc38f32196.note: CDC's current published headline estimate; methodology traces to 2011 (Scallan et al.), not a recent study.

b4a19aed-f8e7-4e34-812d-cecd8ae8a23f.name: CDC 2025 narrower foodborne illness estimate (7 pathogens, 2019 data)
b4a19aed-f8e7-4e34-812d-cecd8ae8a23f.note: A separate, narrower 2025 CDC update covering 7 major pathogens only (~9.9M illnesses, ~53,300 hospitalizations, 931 deaths, 2019 data) — does not replace the broader 48M estimate, which also covers unspecified agents.

9de163da-25db-489c-b6c9-30a755bec241.name: GS1 GLN data model standard
9de163da-25db-489c-b6c9-30a755bec241.note: Confirms GS1 GLN as a maintained standard.

f29ea385-f063-431d-834e-a5d7eac523f8.name: GS1 EPCIS and CBV implementation guideline
f29ea385-f063-431d-834e-a5d7eac523f8.note: Confirms GS1 EPCIS as a maintained traceability data standard.

10d4f375-5d74-4952-b95e-e5655ba2aad6.name: GFSI recognition process (benchmarking, not certifying)
10d4f375-5d74-4952-b95e-e5655ba2aad6.note: Confirms GFSI's role as a benchmarking body recognizing schemes like SQF, BRCGS, FSSC 22000, IFS, rather than certifying directly.

221354f6-184a-4347-9632-e5f4510319de.name: FSSC 22000 GFSI recognition confirmation
221354f6-184a-4347-9632-e5f4510319de.note: Confirms FSSC 22000 as a GFSI-recognized certification scheme.

40aa2d70-7490-4cd3-b572-d42b9f506489.name: FD&C Act text and citation
40aa2d70-7490-4cd3-b572-d42b9f506489.note: Confirms enactment year 1938 and the adulteration/misbranding prohibition as FDA's core statutory authority.

fa2343c4-52d5-496b-97e0-4edd5f69c36a.name: FSMA background and full text
fa2343c4-52d5-496b-97e0-4edd5f69c36a.note: Confirms signing date (Jan 4, 2011), preventive-model framing, and the seven foundational implementing rules.

abcd7b91-6b10-4805-905b-b1da46aae541.name: PCHF final rule
abcd7b91-6b10-4805-905b-b1da46aae541.note: Confirms 21 CFR Part 117, 2015 final rule date, PCQI requirement, and exemptions.

07fed08e-e11c-42df-9eec-92305f751546.name: PCAF final rule
07fed08e-e11c-42df-9eec-92305f751546.note: Confirms 21 CFR Part 507, 2015 final rule date.

b849e231-2362-4766-bf73-d105f1277e6f.name: Produce Safety Rule final rule
b849e231-2362-4766-bf73-d105f1277e6f.note: Confirms 21 CFR Part 112 scope, exemptions, and 2015 final rule date.

17a435a4-56f1-4d53-bd60-3f6b34185875.name: FSVP final rule
17a435a4-56f1-4d53-bd60-3f6b34185875.note: Confirms 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart L and 2015 final rule date.

6ba7f74b-eeab-44e4-9b0a-69ace5186859.name: Sanitary Transportation Rule final rule
6ba7f74b-eeab-44e4-9b0a-69ace5186859.note: Confirms 21 CFR Part 1 Subpart O, 2016 final rule (effective June 6, 2016).

abcb7c40-1113-47e2-b919-0b47f3364aba.name: Intentional Adulteration Rule final rule
abcb7c40-1113-47e2-b919-0b47f3364aba.note: Confirms 21 CFR Part 121, 2016 final rule, and the four "actionable process steps" (official term, not "Actionable Process Attacks").

da05f47f-c44b-47f4-bd19-9ef07eda9955.name: FSMA 204 compliance date extension proposal
da05f47f-c44b-47f4-bd19-9ef07eda9955.note: FDA's August 2025 proposal to extend the compliance date by 30 months, to July 20, 2028.

1d3a1d00-95ca-4af0-918f-5b3b8d0e1421.name: Congressional appropriations act directive on FSMA 204 enforcement
1d3a1d00-95ca-4af0-918f-5b3b8d0e1421.note: The 2026 Continuing Appropriations, Agriculture, Legislative Branch, Military Construction and Veterans Affairs, and Extensions Act directed FDA not to enforce the Food Traceability Rule before July 20, 2028, making that the current binding compliance date rather than the original January 20, 2026.

9a7e4ca7-93a7-4812-ba35-5ccbabc61913.name: FMIA statutory text
9a7e4ca7-93a7-4812-ba35-5ccbabc61913.note: Confirms 1906 enactment and USDA FSIS as the implementing agency.

451f2391-1c63-47a4-a556-7e4e73b64e37.name: PPIA statutory text
451f2391-1c63-47a4-a556-7e4e73b64e37.note: Confirms 1957 enactment and scope (chicken, turkey, duck, other domesticated poultry).

002b2907-b2b9-48fd-a31a-29343c250028.name: EPIA statutory text and FSIS egg products guidance
002b2907-b2b9-48fd-a31a-29343c250028.note: Confirms 1970 enactment and FDA/USDA jurisdiction split (shell eggs vs. egg products).

3de2e495-0a34-4d28-bd26-b0da95272e79.name: FPLA statutory text and FTC administration
3de2e495-0a34-4d28-bd26-b0da95272e79.note: Confirms 1966 enactment and joint FDA/FTC administration split.

cbbd031f-2050-4b54-b8fe-2dbe54d731d4.name: NLEA legislative history
cbbd031f-2050-4b54-b8fe-2dbe54d731d4.note: Confirms 1990 enactment; implementing regulations followed in 1993 with the Nutrition Facts panel required starting 1994.

32f43a5d-b00d-4c31-9899-39e364919865.name: FALCPA statutory text and FASTER Act sesame amendment
32f43a5d-b00d-4c31-9899-39e364919865.note: Confirms 2004 enactment, the 8 original major allergens, and the FASTER Act's 2021 sesame addition (effective Jan 1, 2023).

cf86e662-dcc8-4393-9b08-8d9ec48442d1.name: Bioterrorism Act statutory text and FDA facility registration guide
cf86e662-dcc8-4393-9b08-8d9ec48442d1.note: Confirms 2002 enactment; biennial registration renewal is an FSMA (2011) amendment to FD&C Act § 415(a)(3), not part of the original 2002 Act.

d6431e96-3fef-4372-92a0-1b8d058a44ae.name: "One-up, one-back" recordkeeping terminology
d6431e96-3fef-4372-92a0-1b8d058a44ae.note: Confirms the official term is "one-up, one-back," not "one-up/one-down": record immediate previous source and immediate subsequent recipient.

5650c548-99c2-4084-8475-12768273c73d.name: Meat/poultry HACCP final rule
5650c548-99c2-4084-8475-12768273c73d.note: Confirms 9 CFR Part 417 and the 1996 "Pathogen Reduction/HACCP Systems" final rule date.

4bf11242-7644-408a-b6ce-d0de44084569.name: Seafood HACCP final rule
4bf11242-7644-408a-b6ce-d0de44084569.note: Confirms 21 CFR Part 123, final rule published Dec 18, 1995, effective Dec 18, 1997.

43dbde62-39be-4162-9845-3fcb640f768f.name: Juice HACCP final rule
43dbde62-39be-4162-9845-3fcb640f768f.note: Confirms 21 CFR Part 120, 2001 final rule, phased effective dates through 2004.

6970bad7-82d6-4df0-8a27-590bffc32cbc.name: NBFDS final rule and legislative history
6970bad7-82d6-4df0-8a27-590bffc32cbc.note: Confirms enactment 2016 (amending the Agricultural Marketing Act of 1946), final rule 2018, mandatory compliance Jan 1, 2022 — corrects the record's original single "2022" year, which conflated three distinct dates.

19b68aea-9a1a-4995-8c33-09070414d87f.name: NOP final rule and labeling tiers
19b68aea-9a1a-4995-8c33-09070414d87f.note: Confirms statutory basis (Organic Foods Production Act of 1990), final rule 2000, effective 2002, and the three labeling tiers exactly as described.

09e0ee20-5e5b-4fbf-b222-4f3347f347a7.name: IoT/wireless temperature probe use in cold-chain food monitoring
09e0ee20-5e5b-4fbf-b222-4f3347f347a7.note: Confirms wireless/IoT temperature sensors are standard for HACCP CCP monitoring, receiving, thawing, and cold-chain transport.

d5d20924-e7bf-4c71-8293-4f22e6059606.name: pH control for acidified foods (FDA botulinum target)
d5d20924-e7bf-4c71-8293-4f22e6059606.note: FDA Compliance Program Manual 7303.070 confirms pH below 4.6 as the standard target to control C. botulinum in acidified foods.

b118b0bf-0fcf-4cf9-9b87-993e4f18604d.name: Scale/MES integration for catch-weight and batching
b118b0bf-0fcf-4cf9-9b87-993e4f18604d.note: Well-established, low-risk equipment-use claim; not independently sourced beyond general industry knowledge of MES/batching integration — no red flags found on adjacent searches.

ee4d71d8-e90c-4bbd-a7eb-851b90bb3ac4.name: Allergen cleaning validation — ELISA vs. lateral-flow immunoassay
ee4d71d8-e90c-4bbd-a7eb-851b90bb3ac4.note: Confirms immunoassay-based allergen detection; rapid lateral-flow format (not full lab ELISA) is what's typically used at-line for changeover verification.

19b10a49-1d12-4222-9103-25859cefd368.name: ATP bioluminescence cleaning verification
19b10a49-1d12-4222-9103-25859cefd368.note: Confirms ATP+luciferase bioluminescence gives rapid pass/fail cleaning verification; thresholds are facility-established, not a universal external standard.

160c225b-f836-4037-8cf2-fe54b3b1b3f1.name: Sanitizer chemistry classes and cleaning-before-sanitizing practice
160c225b-f836-4037-8cf2-fe54b3b1b3f1.note: Confirms chlorine/quats/PAA as standard sanitizer classes and that organic/soil load reduces sanitizer efficacy if cleaning is skipped.

dbc3414e-069f-4fbd-ab52-6ce551a7b53e.name: Metal detection as a common CCP
dbc3414e-069f-4fbd-ab52-6ce551a7b53e.note: Confirms metal detectors as standard inline physical-hazard CCPs, especially for ground/chopped products.

ea02b52a-37bd-4fc0-bd76-16581f340071.name: X-ray vs. metal detection sensitivity by material type
ea02b52a-37bd-4fc0-bd76-16581f340071.note: Confirms X-ray's density-contrast detection advantage over metal detection for glass, dense plastics, and bone.

045e2081-03ba-4d0d-8afd-be2563b62663.name: Vision inspection systems for label/code verification
045e2081-03ba-4d0d-8afd-be2563b62663.note: Confirms camera-based vision systems for label presence/alignment and lot-code legibility (OCR/OCV) at line speed.

160cb14f-303a-43ca-8346-2bddaa680cb8.name: Food-manufacturing ERP platform naming (Aptean Ross/Deacom)
160cb14f-303a-43ca-8346-2bddaa680cb8.note: Confirms Deacom is now marketed as Aptean Deacom ERP, and Ross ERP as Aptean Process Manufacturing ERP – Ross Edition — both Aptean-owned process-manufacturing ERP lines, not independent brands.

7b607a72-96cf-4ec0-97c8-59d37cf6d938.name: MES platform rebrands (AVEVA/Wonderware, Plex/Rockwell)
7b607a72-96cf-4ec0-97c8-59d37cf6d938.note: Confirms Wonderware fully rebranded to AVEVA MES, and Plex Systems was acquired by Rockwell Automation (2022), now marketed as Plex MES.

0c8fa46a-d881-4be6-8fc3-55d803fe5df4.name: WMS platform rebrand (Körber/HighJump → Infios) and Fishbowl tier correction
0c8fa46a-d881-4be6-8fc3-55d803fe5df4.note: Confirms Körber's supply-chain software unit (including former HighJump) rebranded to Infios (March 2025). Fishbowl was removed from this list — confirmed as SMB-tier inventory software, not enterprise-grade WMS comparable to Manhattan/Blue Yonder/SAP EWM.

9ea3e8c2-c580-4f66-a446-3c9f9104cc1d.name: Intelex and Cority are separate, competing companies
9ea3e8c2-c580-4f66-a446-3c9f9104cc1d.note: Corrects the prior "Intelex (CORITY)" entry — Intelex and Cority are unrelated, competing EHSQ software companies, not a merged entity. Listed separately.

49663ec7-cffb-4c54-b89f-3a2bebc3c2b2.name: LIMS role in food QA sample/result management
49663ec7-cffb-4c54-b89f-3a2bebc3c2b2.note: Confirms LIMS's standard role: sample intake/tracking, test result capture, comparison against specification limits, hold/release workflows.

eb48e77e-26fe-4872-8960-93ee98d0db25.name: Trustwell/FoodLogiQ rebrand
eb48e77e-26fe-4872-8960-93ee98d0db25.note: Confirms FoodLogiQ merged with ESHA Research (Oct 2022) and rebranded to Trustwell (Feb 2023); FoodLogiQ persists as a legacy product-line name.

83d0e275-932a-453e-be0a-651cd1bf6d34.name: rfxcel acquisition by Antares Vision Group
83d0e275-932a-453e-be0a-651cd1bf6d34.note: Confirms rfXcel was acquired by Antares Vision Group (2021); still operates as a named product/brand under that parent.

57a5b52b-4ea4-4feb-ab23-13dcf3907ee2.name: IBM Food Trust current active status (2026 direct check)
57a5b52b-4ea4-4feb-ab23-13dcf3907ee2.note: Directly re-checked given known IBM blockchain-platform sunset history (e.g. TradeLens, 2022-2023): no discontinuation/divestment announcement found. IBM's Food Trust product docs remain live, and IBM/iFoodDS announced a new joint FSMA-204-focused solution in 2024/2025, indicating continued investment rather than sunset.

68e8cd24-422d-4aaa-9e41-4787e40ef760.name: Label management platform categorization corrections
68e8cd24-422d-4aaa-9e41-4787e40ef760.note: Removed Label Traxx (converter/printer-side ERP, not brand-side label compliance tooling, post-Amtech acquisition) and Nulogy (contract-packaging/CPG supply-chain collaboration software, not a label-creation/artwork tool) from this list as category mismatches for a brand-side label management system.

0e3f3b1c-c41a-486e-9fdb-691f542734cb.name: Advanced Planning & Scheduling (APS) in food/beverage manufacturing
0e3f3b1c-c41a-486e-9fdb-691f542734cb.note: Confirms APS category functionality: capacity optimization, changeover/allergen-changeover sequencing, raw material availability, shelf-life constraints, delivery deadlines, ERP integration.

a6bb949d-d869-4c5d-8de7-bb74e2859b5e.name: Formulation/recipe management system category functionality
a6bb949d-d869-4c5d-8de7-bb74e2859b5e.note: Confirms version-controlled BOM/recipe as source of truth for nutrition calculation, allergen content, and MRP is standard across vendors in this category.

7c8a187b-4b6c-4bc1-9399-436bff7668d7.name: EDI X12 transaction set numbering (850/855/856/810/940/945)
7c8a187b-4b6c-4bc1-9399-436bff7668d7.note: Confirms all six transaction-set number mappings: 850 Purchase Order, 855 PO Acknowledgment, 856 Advance Ship Notice, 810 Invoice, 940 Warehouse Shipping Order, 945 Warehouse Shipping Advice.

ce2ee7b9-8a7f-416e-a0b4-5977277d202d.name: Supplier qualification questionnaire — standard industry practice
ce2ee7b9-8a7f-416e-a0b4-5977277d202d.note: Generic supplier-qualification process document; no single external standard to cite, consistent with standard supplier onboarding practice.

604ca7e5-697b-4266-8669-4bee3c0da479.name: Structured COA data schema — original design
604ca7e5-697b-4266-8669-4bee3c0da479.note: Original data-model design for representing COA contents as structured, comparable data; not itself a named external standard, though it mirrors how Material/Product Specifications are already commonly structured.

6e99f18a-4010-42d5-825e-13343930da2a.name: Supplier risk assessment record — standard industry practice
6e99f18a-4010-42d5-825e-13343930da2a.note: Generic qualification-review process document; no single external standard to cite.

7bba9c5e-beb9-4663-905c-1f234c1a6032.name: FSMA Preventive Controls supplier audit frequency baseline
7bba9c5e-beb9-4663-905c-1f234c1a6032.note: 21 CFR 117.410/117.425 requires an onsite audit before first use and at least annually for suppliers controlling a serious hazard, with no fixed tiered numeric schedule of its own — the artifact's specific 6-12/12-24/24-36 month bands are this design's own policy, not a regulatory citation.

44c30246-80de-4c2b-9969-1097973d6151.name: Supplier risk-tiering bands — original design, not a codified standard
44c30246-80de-4c2b-9969-1097973d6151.note: The specific audit-frequency-by-risk-tier ranges (6-12/12-24/24-36 months) are common-sense internal policy design, explicitly not traceable to any authoritative FSMA/GFSI source — flagged during the item 9a audit as invented specificity that must not be presented as codified. Recorded honestly as subject-matter judgment per content-standards.md.

dee1afd1-994e-4c2b-a12e-4e044ffb0680.name: GFSI scheme audit/re-certification cadence
dee1afd1-994e-4c2b-a12e-4e044ffb0680.note: GFSI-recognized schemes (SQF, BRCGS, FSSC 22000) require annual surveillance audits, with full re-certification typically every 3 years for schemes like FSSC 22000/BRCGS.

aa2411fc-8532-48e3-850d-1de626c802ca.name: Supplier certification record — standard industry practice
aa2411fc-8532-48e3-850d-1de626c802ca.note: Generic certification-tracking document; no single external standard to cite.

0b633476-f77a-412c-8b5f-907c85574052.name: Material/Product Specification — standard industry practice
0b633476-f77a-412c-8b5f-907c85574052.note: Generic specification document practice; no single external standard to cite.

56688231-e5c4-463f-a766-450a750a4cbb.name: Lab test result record — standard industry practice
56688231-e5c4-463f-a766-450a750a4cbb.note: Generic lab-result recordkeeping; no single external standard to cite.

b5ee7707-1e5a-4a6b-b50a-de955b74dbe7.name: Approved Supplier List — standard industry practice
b5ee7707-1e5a-4a6b-b50a-de955b74dbe7.note: Generic procurement-control document; no single external standard to cite.

4a1aaaef-b5d7-42bd-a002-0fb72f11ba1f.name: Certificate of Analysis — standard industry document
4a1aaaef-b5d7-42bd-a002-0fb72f11ba1f.note: COAs are a standard, well-established supply-chain document; no single external standard governs their issuance.

c2495ee1-d83e-48cc-8785-279addb18dc8.name: Supplier performance KPI thresholds — typical industry convention
c2495ee1-d83e-48cc-8785-279addb18dc8.note: Good/Fair/Poor thresholds (e.g. on-time >=95%) are typical industry KPI conventions, not tied to a specific regulation or named standard.

f6b36ec8-7953-4dd1-9f5a-c537de25b5b6.name: EDI 856 Advance Ship Notice transaction set
f6b36ec8-7953-4dd1-9f5a-c537de25b5b6.note: Confirms EDI 856 as the correct ANSI X12 transaction set for Advance Ship Notice.

6a7bbb14-bec6-4f1a-a7d1-1e59a5cc4a91.name: Receiving log — standard industry practice
6a7bbb14-bec6-4f1a-a7d1-1e59a5cc4a91.note: Generic dock-receipt recordkeeping; no single external standard to cite.

bdcb6135-702b-4188-928a-36e775c7c786.name: Temperature record — standard cold-chain practice
bdcb6135-702b-4188-928a-36e775c7c786.note: Generic cold-chain recordkeeping practice; no single external standard to cite beyond general cold-chain evidence norms.

1b5be89c-635d-4dad-98aa-280b1ed1bca2.name: Receiving inspection record — standard industry practice
1b5be89c-635d-4dad-98aa-280b1ed1bca2.note: Generic incoming-inspection recordkeeping; no single external standard to cite.

1a80f32f-5ac1-4d89-b208-1804b73889f9.name: Hold/quarantine tag — standard industry practice
1a80f32f-5ac1-4d89-b208-1804b73889f9.note: Generic material-hold control; no single external standard to cite.

cd89af6a-36cc-4b0a-9b7d-a3b139ac0691.name: FEFO (First-Expired-First-Out) as standard food-industry inventory term
cd89af6a-36cc-4b0a-9b7d-a3b139ac0691.note: Confirms FEFO (not FIFO) as the correct, standard term for perishable-goods inventory management prioritizing by expiration date.

95359c9c-3a50-46a2-a9c6-f33336343bef.name: Allergen matrix — standard industry practice
95359c9c-3a50-46a2-a9c6-f33336343bef.note: Generic allergen-tracking document; no single external standard to cite.

a43f873f-01f6-489b-ab25-293f6dc04bbe.name: Allergen sequencing algorithm — original design
a43f873f-01f6-489b-ab25-293f6dc04bbe.note: Formalizes the widely-taught "fewer to more allergens" production-scheduling heuristic into an original subset-ordering algorithm; the specific formalization is original engineering design, not a citation to a named external methodology.

3edca15f-02d0-4a7e-86a7-90ff6b9948f7.name: Demand forecast — standard industry practice
3edca15f-02d0-4a7e-86a7-90ff6b9948f7.note: Generic demand-planning recordkeeping; no single external standard to cite.

b3531b59-2526-4a74-9981-52518ffb046e.name: FD&C Act ingredient statement accuracy requirement
b3531b59-2526-4a74-9981-52518ffb046e.note: FD&C Act misbranding provisions require the label's ingredient statement to accurately reflect actual formulation on an ongoing basis; "at all times" is an operational paraphrase of a continuous compliance obligation, not a literal regulatory phrase.

14f29ec6-4a41-4610-bedd-fe3d71366556.name: Master Production Schedule — standard industry practice
14f29ec6-4a41-4610-bedd-fe3d71366556.note: Generic production-planning recordkeeping; no single external standard to cite.

1c23a6e5-8174-41ca-a87e-e2918784512d.name: EDI 850/855 Purchase Order and Acknowledgment transaction sets
1c23a6e5-8174-41ca-a87e-e2918784512d.note: Confirms 850 (Purchase Order) and 855 (PO Acknowledgment) as the correct ANSI X12 transaction set numbers.

49fdc4f2-7589-449f-bb8f-819760907c7a.name: Production/work order — standard industry practice
49fdc4f2-7589-449f-bb8f-819760907c7a.note: Generic shop-floor release document; no single external standard to cite.

e8a1740f-7e51-4b29-8c28-2c799b0bf19e.name: Batch/production record — standard traceability concept
e8a1740f-7e51-4b29-8c28-2c799b0bf19e.note: Core traceability recordkeeping concept, standard across the industry; no single external standard governs its exact shape.

7319a19d-d6f5-4b24-8ba9-679a2866ecd1.name: Line clearance record — standard industry practice
7319a19d-d6f5-4b24-8ba9-679a2866ecd1.note: Generic pre-run verification document; no single external standard to cite.

f57aebef-5e5f-4b24-a520-d07e5d0b6cae.name: HACCP plan revalidation cadence — FSIS vs. FDA PCHF
f57aebef-5e5f-4b24-a520-d07e5d0b6cae.note: 9 CFR 417.4(a)(3)(i) requires USDA FSIS meat/poultry HACCP reassessment at least annually and on hazard-affecting change. FDA-regulated facilities under PCHF (21 CFR 117.170) instead require reanalysis at least every 3 years plus triggered reanalysis — a different cadence that the artifact now distinguishes explicitly.

be484943-4fb9-4c8d-b55e-4d648e610674.name: 165°F cook CCP critical limit example (FSIS poultry lethality)
be484943-4fb9-4c8d-b55e-4d648e610674.note: Confirms 165°F internal temperature as a real, widely used FSIS critical-limit example for poultry cooking Salmonella lethality.

0a8d44b5-f92e-4a4a-87a9-71fe6193e2ea.name: CCP monitoring log — core HACCP concept
0a8d44b5-f92e-4a4a-87a9-71fe6193e2ea.note: Standard HACCP monitoring-evidence concept, consistent with FDA/USDA HACCP frameworks generally; no single citation beyond the CCP Definition's own sources.

e1d01769-cbc3-4550-932a-a42fb37ecab6.name: Corrective action record — core HACCP concept
e1d01769-cbc3-4550-932a-a42fb37ecab6.note: Standard HACCP corrective-action concept, consistent with FDA/USDA HACCP frameworks generally.

c90f8576-1ae4-4165-bc67-f1e079a3821c.name: Allergen changeover cleaning record — standard industry practice
c90f8576-1ae4-4165-bc67-f1e079a3821c.note: Generic allergen-changeover verification record; no single external standard to cite beyond the ELISA/ATP test-kit sources already recorded on the Materials collection.

c4fb115e-64bf-4a2e-98cf-884f2f8c9e2b.name: Master Sanitation Schedule — standard GMP prerequisite program
c4fb115e-64bf-4a2e-98cf-884f2f8c9e2b.note: Standard GMP prerequisite-program concept; no single external standard to cite beyond general GMP practice.

88d46ab3-c98c-4466-aebd-74d899ce0448.name: Environmental monitoring record — standard industry practice
88d46ab3-c98c-4466-aebd-74d899ce0448.note: Generic swab-event recordkeeping; specific zone concept sourced separately on the EMP Zone & Trending Standard artifact.

fcbe4d9b-a7be-40b4-9ac3-eb36ac4b2ae9.name: FDA draft guidance on Listeria control in RTE foods (EMP zone model)
fcbe4d9b-a7be-40b4-9ac3-eb36ac4b2ae9.note: Confirms the 4-zone EMP model and "search and destroy" terminology are consistent with FDA draft guidance; specific numeric escalation triggers (2 consecutive positives / 3 clean to close) are common industry practice, not FDA-codified thresholds — FDA's guidance emphasizes root-cause investigation over rigid escalation counts.

fab381b3-e817-4657-b53b-9fc161b5c224.name: SPC control chart — standard statistical process control concept
fab381b3-e817-4657-b53b-9fc161b5c224.note: Standard SPC concept (out-of-control vs. out-of-spec); not tied to a single external citation.

e742f55c-2d62-4c07-bd63-e960ed8e7580.name: Nonconformance record — standard industry practice
e742f55c-2d62-4c07-bd63-e960ed8e7580.note: Generic OOS/nonconformance recordkeeping; no single external standard to cite.

e9cbd6aa-f86b-42b9-a9ac-754af4cb3549.name: CAPA record — standard quality-management concept
e9cbd6aa-f86b-42b9-a9ac-754af4cb3549.note: Standard QMS CAPA concept (5 Whys/fishbone, effectiveness verification); generic industry practice, not tied to one regulation.

2ab48fe9-23c5-4743-a5cf-fd07252ef54b.name: Catch weight record — standard industry practice
2ab48fe9-23c5-4743-a5cf-fd07252ef54b.note: Generic catch-weight capture recordkeeping; external mechanism sourced on the Catch-Weight Data Model artifact.

9303e717-7e74-4902-a12c-2e52967c09a2.name: GS1 variable-measure GTIN and GS1-128 Application Identifiers for net weight
9303e717-7e74-4902-a12c-2e52967c09a2.note: Confirms the GS1 variable-measure GTIN prefix and GS1-128 AI (310n)/(320n) mechanism for carrying actual/catch weight through label and supply-chain data flows.

22944f8c-b59b-47e6-8b84-a03d1b0b9f47.name: Package integrity inspection record — standard industry practice
22944f8c-b59b-47e6-8b84-a03d1b0b9f47.note: Generic inspection-event recordkeeping; equipment sources already recorded on Materials collection (Metal Detector, X-Ray Inspection System).

3092edf5-03ac-4bd1-80dd-b9ab6d25262e.name: Label copy draft — standard industry practice
3092edf5-03ac-4bd1-80dd-b9ab6d25262e.note: Generic label-drafting workflow; no single external standard to cite.

20fe8002-7946-4835-9ab0-a532bd8fa294.name: Label compliance checklist — standard industry practice
20fe8002-7946-4835-9ab0-a532bd8fa294.note: Generic manual-review checklist; specific rule citations sourced on the Label Compliance Check Catalog artifact.

76aa2fc5-1b0f-4b8b-a5c9-9c81d57f1135.name: FDA nutrient-content claim thresholds and gluten-free ppm threshold
76aa2fc5-1b0f-4b8b-a5c9-9c81d57f1135.note: Confirms and corrects citations: 21 CFR 101.54 governs "good source of" claims (10-19% DV/serving), 21 CFR 101.62 governs "low fat" (not 101.54 as previously implied), 21 CFR 101.61 governs "reduced sodium" (not 101.56, which governs "light"/"lite"), and 21 CFR 101.91 confirms the gluten-free <20ppm attestation threshold.

2922e870-10ec-47cd-b719-fe8c401f4166.name: Label artwork file — standard industry practice
2922e870-10ec-47cd-b719-fe8c401f4166.note: Generic artwork version-control workflow; no single external standard to cite.

517d1350-79d2-440f-9e53-0cdd9777473a.name: FSIS generic label approval — pre-approval now the exception
517d1350-79d2-440f-9e53-0cdd9777473a.note: Confirms FSIS pre-approval is no longer the default: since 2013 and expanded by a 2023 final rule, most labels self-certify under generic approval; pre-approval now applies only to temporary approvals, religious exempt labels, and special-claim labels.

de0131ba-0016-43c5-be42-b93590657a4b.name: Lot/date code record — standard industry practice
de0131ba-0016-43c5-be42-b93590657a4b.note: Generic lot-coding recordkeeping; no single external standard to cite.

609c4eca-7daf-4f64-a6a3-3c7c3efe6c85.name: Label application verification record — standard industry practice
609c4eca-7daf-4f64-a6a3-3c7c3efe6c85.note: Generic in-line QA check recordkeeping; vision-system source already recorded on Materials collection.

4c31a30a-41ae-4bd5-8b40-ccbd865b6521.name: Shelf-life record — standard industry/customer-contract practice
4c31a30a-41ae-4bd5-8b40-ccbd865b6521.note: Customer minimum-remaining-shelf-life requirements are common industry/contract practice, not a single codified external standard.

cfc228c5-177e-43ab-bb38-3abea6efd3e7.name: FSMA 204 Shipping Critical Tracking Event
cfc228c5-177e-43ab-bb38-3abea6efd3e7.note: Confirms Shipping as one of the Critical Tracking Events defined at 21 CFR 1.1340.

0edca96f-32ec-43c5-917e-f94d790820f0.name: FSMA 204 Shipping KDEs — corrected field list (21 CFR 1.1340)
0edca96f-32ec-43c5-917e-f94d790820f0.note: Corrects the prior KDE list: transporter identity is NOT a required KDE (the rule explicitly excludes transporters from the recipient-location KDE), and GTIN/GLN are optional implementation choices, not mandated formats. Actual required KDEs: traceability lot code, product description, quantity/UOM, ship-to/ship-from location descriptions, ship date, TLC source information, reference document type/number.

d6ae1a5e-a73d-4a25-bdf6-c9ca43e71ea2.name: FSMA 204 Traceability Lot Code assignment rule (21 CFR 1.1320)
d6ae1a5e-a73d-4a25-bdf6-c9ca43e71ea2.note: Confirms a new TLC is assigned only at initial packing (non-seafood raw ag commodity), first land-based receiving from a fishing vessel, or transformation — not at other CTEs like shipping.

b70fd9e6-8c23-4609-bb25-0cdd839edd76.name: GS1 EPCIS core model and FSMA 204 positioning
b70fd9e6-8c23-4609-bb25-0cdd839edd76.note: Confirms EPCIS's "what/where/when/why" core dimensions and GS1 US's guidance mapping EPCIS 2.0 onto FSMA 204 CTEs/KDEs — industry-promoted, not FDA-mandated.

6887f31d-a76f-4c46-8aa1-dc7b30c47b27.name: OSH Act of 1970, Section 5(a) — General Duty Clause
6887f31d-a76f-4c46-8aa1-dc7b30c47b27.note: Establishes OSHA's general duty clause requiring employers to provide a workplace free of recognized hazards; statutory basis for OSHA's applicability to food manufacturing plants.

086b4e33-b8da-4f0b-a2df-e5b94639a8c6.name: OSHA Process Safety Management standard — ammonia refrigeration applicability
086b4e33-b8da-4f0b-a2df-e5b94639a8c6.note: PSM applies to facilities with anhydrous ammonia refrigeration inventories at or above the 10,000 lb threshold, common in food cold-storage/finished-goods warehousing.

6df5fcc5-2076-421a-9545-d8a56ae4fcdc.name: FDA Nutrition Facts Label revision final rule (2016)
6df5fcc5-2076-421a-9545-d8a56ae4fcdc.citation: 81 FR 33742 (May 27, 2016); 21 CFR 101.9; compliance dates extended by 83 FR 19619 (2018)
6df5fcc5-2076-421a-9545-d8a56ae4fcdc.note: Added-sugars declaration, updated Daily Values, revised serving sizes, larger/bolder calorie type. Compliance dates: Jan 1, 2020 (manufacturers with $10M+ annual food sales), Jan 1, 2021 (smaller manufacturers).

3f0518be-803d-4760-8c85-7b0bec95624f.name: FDA proposed front-of-package nutrition labeling rule (2025)
3f0518be-803d-4760-8c85-7b0bec95624f.note: Proposed, not yet final as of this dataset's last check — a mandatory front-of-package 'Nutrition Info Box' covering saturated fat, sodium, and added sugars. Comment period extended to July 15, 2025; FDA's 2026 Human Foods Program priorities target a final rule around spring 2026, still pending as of this dataset's last verification.

0f348398-32a4-4ff9-9c34-90f1fd807870.name: CBP role in FDA-regulated food import entries
0f348398-32a4-4ff9-9c34-90f1fd807870.note: CBP receives and processes entry filings for imported goods including FDA-regulated food, refers entries to FDA for admissibility review, and coordinates with FDA on detention, refusal, and redelivery — a border-enforcement/logistics role distinct from FDA's FSVP (importer-facing hazard-analysis/verification duties).

1fe3a2e7-4cc8-4ec1-b7e5-daa97c3dc2d7.name: Neogen acquisition of Corvium (2023 rebrand to Neogen Analytics)
1fe3a2e7-4cc8-4ec1-b7e5-daa97c3dc2d7.note: Neogen Corporation acquired Corvium Inc.'s food safety software/data analytics business in February 2023; the product is now marketed as Neogen Analytics.

f5b208bb-84ad-4c3a-9f80-47913b447491.name: LIMS vendors serving food & beverage labs
f5b208bb-84ad-4c3a-9f80-47913b447491.note: LabWare, STARLIMS, LabVantage, and Thermo Fisher SampleManager are established LIMS vendors actively marketing to food & beverage testing labs.

8406cc4a-f8f7-4a43-8f9f-e6223711bb31.name: Advanced planning & scheduling vendors serving food & beverage manufacturing
8406cc4a-f8f7-4a43-8f9f-e6223711bb31.note: PlanetTogether (acquired by CAI Software, rebranded CAI PlanetTogether, June 2026) markets allergen-aware, changeover-optimized APS scheduling for food & beverage manufacturers; Siemens Opcenter (formerly Preactor) is also actively used in the industry.

0a0dae24-63cd-4e14-9486-51f03b2e12f5.name: Formulation/recipe-management vendors serving food & beverage
0a0dae24-63cd-4e14-9486-51f03b2e12f5.note: Genesis R&D / ESHA Research (Trustwell) for formulation and nutrition-labeling; Vicinity Software for batch/formula/BOM version management, marketed as complementary to Genesis R&D.

2a1b3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5e.name: Case/pack configuration verification as standard secondary-packaging practice
2a1b3c4d-5e6f-4a7b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5e.note: No single external regulation mandates a case/pack configuration record by this name. Asserted based on standard secondary-packaging practice: case count, case labeling, and outer-carton weight/date verification are routinely logged wherever a QMS or MES governs packaging, as evidenced by this dataset's own sibling steps (Fill & seal has a Batch/Production Record, Catch weight verification has a Catch Weight Record, Package integrity check has an Inspection Record) all of which log an equivalent verification step. Not independently sourced to a named regulation because none applies at this granularity.

3b2c4d5e-6f7a-4b8c-9d0e-1f2a3b4c5d6f.name: FEFO exception/override logging as standard WMS practice
3b2c4d5e-6f7a-4b8c-9d0e-1f2a3b4c5d6f.note: No external regulation mandates a FEFO exception log by this name. Asserted based on standard WMS practice: a system that enforces FEFO picking logic necessarily needs an override/exception path (e.g. a closer-to-expiry pallet blocked by a pick-face location issue), and that override needs to be logged for the same reason any manual departure from system-directed logic needs an audit trail under a food safety/quality management system. Not independently sourced to a named regulation because none applies at this granularity.

beeaf2df-ff74-4740-9ffe-ffec459f7d12.name: NIST Handbook 44 — weighing device calibration standards
beeaf2df-ff74-4740-9ffe-ffec459f7d12.citation: NIST Handbook 44, Specifications, Tolerances, and Other Technical Requirements for Weighing and Measuring Devices
beeaf2df-ff74-4740-9ffe-ffec459f7d12.note: The recognized US standard governing accuracy/calibration requirements for commercial scales; calibration weight sets traceable to NIST reference standards are the accepted means of demonstrating a scale meets it.

10bcb8e9-c28f-4e35-9f7d-1ea885633302.name: 3-A Sanitary Standards for CIP-cleanable equipment
10bcb8e9-c28f-4e35-9f7d-1ea885633302.citation: 3-A Sanitary Standards, Inc. — Accepted Practices for CIP-cleanable equipment/circuits
10bcb8e9-c28f-4e35-9f7d-1ea885633302.note: 3-A Sanitary Standards define design and cleanability requirements for dairy/food processing equipment intended to be cleaned in place, and are the industry-recognized reference for CIP system design in food and beverage manufacturing.

cb31744a-7d69-4379-8f51-2d32122f39ba.name: Sanitizer concentration test-strip verification as standard sanitation practice
cb31744a-7d69-4379-8f51-2d32122f39ba.note: No single regulation mandates test-strip verification by name — only the sanitization outcome is regulated. Confirming mixed sanitizer concentration before use via test strips is standard, widely documented sanitation practice (sold by major sanitation chemical suppliers for this purpose), asserted honestly as subject-matter-judgment.

849bbfe5-b4df-4625-b515-53e6d75e7551.name: PCHF equipment maintenance requirement
849bbfe5-b4df-4625-b515-53e6d75e7551.note: Requires plant equipment be adequately maintained; grounds the mechanical/electrical upkeep side of equipment-dependent CCP and process-control steps, distinct from measurement-accuracy calibration.

22fa4bf3-b797-4f1b-be0d-a1342f9b2f8c.name: PCHF qualified-individual and personnel training requirement
22fa4bf3-b797-4f1b-be0d-a1342f9b2f8c.note: 117.4(b)(1) requires individuals performing preventive-control-related duties be qualified through training/experience; 117.180 covers the PCQI training requirement for the food safety plan itself. Grounds the general training-record obligation.

c0849b4a-48dd-404e-bc40-c801459540fc.name: Documented competency records extended to operator-level duties
c0849b4a-48dd-404e-bc40-c801459540fc.note: Extending a documented-competency-record principle to specific operator-level duties named in this dataset (CCP monitoring, line clearance, batching) beyond the narrower PCQI role is this design's own reasonable extension of PCHF's qualified-individual principle, not a direct per-role regulatory mandate — flagged honestly rather than force-citing a rule that doesn't name these roles specifically.

5a0c77cc-b360-4d0e-8ecc-5bc412384ae6.name: Training/LMS as a distinct plant system category
5a0c77cc-b360-4d0e-8ecc-5bc412384ae6.note: Alchemy (Intertek) and ComplianceMate are real, currently marketed training/LMS-capable platforms in food manufacturing; distinguishing Training/LMS as its own system category from QMS document control is a reasonable but not externally mandated organizational choice.

d34a2e5f-a618-483f-a8f6-42a1d6d9e156.name: ISA-95 (IEC 62264) enterprise-control system integration hierarchy
d34a2e5f-a618-483f-a8f6-42a1d6d9e156.note: Defines the standard Level 2 (SCADA/basic control) vs. Level 3 (MES) functional split, grounding SCADA/HMI as a distinct layer from MES.

56c9cddc-092d-4c21-b006-bd6604d3dd42.name: BRCGS Global Standard for Food Safety, Issue 9 publication
56c9cddc-092d-4c21-b006-bd6604d3dd42.citation: BRCGS Global Standard for Food Safety, Issue 9
56c9cddc-092d-4c21-b006-bd6604d3dd42.note: BRCGS's own announcement of Issue 9's publication (August 2022) and certification timeline (audits from Feb 1, 2023).

16daba2d-f77e-4992-ab4a-f4793abacb52.name: IFS Food Standard Version 8 GFSI recognition
16daba2d-f77e-4992-ab4a-f4793abacb52.note: IFS's own announcement confirming Version 8 became mandatory Jan 1, 2024, and achieved GFSI recognition against the 2020 GFSI Benchmarking Requirements on Sept 16, 2024.
